description: Modify a modulation signal using a lookup table

parameterGroups.label: Input value
parameterGroups.rationale: The normalised input value (0..1) used as the lookup position in the table.

gaps.question: Confirm that cable_table uses the standard table lookup pattern: index::lerp<index::normalised<double, index::clamped<512>>> with linear interpolation. Does the lookup produce a 0..1 output from the table values?
gaps.context: The index-types infrastructure doc describes the standard table lookup pattern. Need to confirm cable_table uses this exact pattern.

gaps.question: Does cable_table call setDisplayedValue() to show the current lookup position in the table UI?
gaps.context: Visual feedback of the current lookup position is a common UX feature for table nodes.

gaps.question: What happens when the table data is empty or not connected? Does the node output 0, pass through the input, or produce undefined behaviour?
gaps.context: Users need to know the default behaviour before editing the table.

notes: cable_table is a value transformation node (Pattern 2 from control-infrastructure) that uses a 512-float lookup table to reshape a control signal. Input is normalised (0..1), table lookup with linear interpolation maps it to a new value, output is also normalised. This is the control-rate equivalent of core.table (which operates on audio signals). The index-types doc confirms the standard pattern: index::lerp<index::normalised<double, index::clamped<SAMPLE_LOOKUP_TABLE_SIZE>>>.
